info.title: kysigned API
info.description: Self-verifying e-signatures: create an envelope from a PDF plus signers; each human signer signs by forwarding one email (their provider DKIM-signs it); every party receives an offline-verifiable evidence bundle. This document describes the public /v1 surface of a kysigned instance. Machine-readable errors: every error response is `{ "error": string, "code": string }` where `code` is a stable taxonomy value (`auth_*`, `csrf_*`, `payment_*`, `validation_*`, `state_*`, `not_found`, `rate_size_*`, `idempotency_*`, `internal_*`). Completion webhooks: pass `callback_url` at create; deliveries are signed with `X-Kysigned-Signature: t=<unix>,v1=<hex hmac-sha256(callback_secret, "<t>.<rawBody>")>` — verify by recomputing the HMAC over `t + "." + rawBody` with the `callback_secret` returned once in the create response, comparing constant-time, and rejecting timestamps outside your tolerance window.

apiKey.description: A creator API key (`ksk_…`), minted in the dashboard at /account/api-keys. Scoped to the creator envelope actions; CSRF-exempt. Also accepted bare (without the `Bearer ` prefix).

session.name: kysigned_session
session.description: The browser session cookie (magic-link or passkey sign-in). Unsafe methods additionally require the `X-Kysigned-Csrf` header.

trackingToken.description: An envelope-scoped, READ-ONLY tracking token (`ktt_…`) returned in every create response's `tracking.token` (F-30.7). Sent in the Authorization header (bare or `Bearer`), it authorizes GET /v1/envelope/{id} for exactly its own envelope — no account, API key, or session. Any other envelope id answers 404; every mutating/listing/download route refuses it (403 auth_tracking_scope).

code.description: Stable machine-readable taxonomy code (auth_*, csrf_*, payment_*, validation_*, state_*, not_found, rate_size_*, idempotency_*, internal_*)

name.description: Display name; blank falls back to the email

on_behalf_of.description: Organisation this signer signs on behalf of

pdf_base64.description: Base64 PDF, ≤ ~3 MB raw (synchronous-invoke payload cap). Use pdf_url for larger documents.

callback_url.description: https-only completion-webhook URL. The 201 response returns callback_secret (once) for verifying the X-Kysigned-Signature header on deliveries.

auto_close.description: false = manual seal (POST /v1/envelope/{id}/seal) after all signers sign

creator_email.description: The creator record this paid envelope attaches to — a deliverable address (creation/completion mail and the evidence bundle land there). No prior account needed; sign in later with this email (magic link / passkey) to see the envelope and the wallet-sourced ledger entries in the dashboard.

link.description: Programmatic signer-token API base (404s in a browser by design)

review_link.description: Human, browser-openable review page (same URL the invite email embeds)

callback_secret.description: whs_… webhook signing secret — returned EXACTLY ONCE, only when callback_url was supplied

delivery.description: Per-signer send outcome (delivered / undeliverable / failed counts)

tracking.description: The envelope-observer handle (returned on EVERY create). `token` (ktt_…) is an envelope-scoped READ-ONLY credential: present it as the Authorization on GET /v1/envelope/{id} to poll status (full signer roster + delivery) with no account, API key, or session. It reads exactly this one envelope — any other id answers 404, and every mutation/list/download refuses it (403 auth_tracking_scope). Lost after a restart? The free POST /v1/envelope/preflight spending-intent replay (creator_email + your idempotency_key) returns this same create body, token included. Valid while the envelope exists (completion and void stay readable); dies with retention expiry or account deletion.

poll.description: Runnable poll instruction for the token

delivery_status.description: pending | delivered | undeliverable — whether the signing-request email reached the signer (distinct from signing status)

post.summary: Email the creator a one-time sign-in link
Exchange a magic-link token for a creator session cookie
Create an envelope (PDF + signers); emails each signing request

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Retry-safe create: the same key replays the original 201 (one envelope, one debit). Same key + different payload → 409 idempotency_key_reuse.

post.summary: Machine-paid create (x402): a wallet pays the flat per-envelope price — no account, no key, no session
post.description: Operator-dependent (enabled on kysigned.com; a fork without x402 config answers 404 payment_x402_not_enabled). An UNPAID call is challenged by the platform with a standard x402 402 (payment requirements: the per-envelope price, the operator's payout wallet, mainnet networks) — that challenge is the x402 protocol shape, not this API's error envelope. An x402-capable client pays and retries; the platform verifies and settles BEFORE this endpoint runs. The settled payment credits the creator's ledger exactly once (keyed on the stable payment id) and the create debits it, so a same-proof retry replays the SAME 201 — one payment, one envelope. The body is the standard create body PLUS the required creator_email. Optional Idempotency-Key header: send your own spending-intent key so a retry with the same key replays the same envelope (else dedupe is keyed on the settled payment id). Discovery: an unfunded POST /v1/envelope names this route and price in its 402 body (x402_route, x402_price_usd_micros).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional caller spending-intent key; a retry with the same key replays the same envelope (create dedupe). Absent → dedupe keys on the settled payment id.

201.description: Created. Identical to POST /v1/envelope PLUS a `payment` receipt (payment_id, network, amount_usd_micros, asset, pay_to, settlement_reference, settled_at) and a merged `tracking` block LED by the ktt_ tracking token — a wallet-only creator polls GET /v1/envelope/{id} with it, no account needed (status_url_auth: creator_or_tracking_token); creator_email sign-in stays available for the full dashboard. Reconcile the spend via `payment.payment_id` / `settlement_reference`.

400.description: Deterministic validation failed (plus-alias signer, bad email, unparseable/oversize PDF, …). If the payment already settled, the response carries `payment_banked: true` + `credit_email` + `next_actions` — the money became recoverable account credit for creator_email, not a loss. Call POST /v1/envelope/preflight (free) BEFORE paying to avoid this.

402.description: x402 payment challenge for an unpaid call — emitted by the platform (x402 protocol payment-requirements shape, not the {error, code} envelope). Pay per the challenge and retry. A paid retry from a wallet with insufficient USDC on Base mainnet returns 402 payment_insufficient_funds with a fund_wallet next_action (network, asset, minimum_amount_usd_micros) — fund the wallet and retry.

post.summary: Validate a create body for FREE (no charge, no create) — check inputs and replay-check a spending intent before paying the x402 create
post.description: Runs the same deterministic create validations (creator_email shape, signer addresses incl. the plus-alias rule, document_name, PDF presence/size/parseability for pdf_base64) without creating or charging. Use it before POST /v1/x402/envelope so a request that would be rejected does not consume the on-chain payment (run402 settles before the app validates). A PASS means these checks won't reject the paid create. creator_email is optional (omit it for a keyed/session create). pdf_url is not fetched here. Spending-intent replay check: include creator_email + the idempotency_key you will send on the paid call — if that intent already produced an envelope, the 200 returns { ok: true, already_created: true, envelope: <the stored create result> } and you MUST NOT pay again (the route is always-priced, so re-sending a completed intent settles a second payment; the money would be banked as credit, not lost, but the right move is to not pay). With a key and no prior envelope the 200 carries already_created: false.

get.summary: Envelope status: per-signer signing state, signed times, delivery_status
get.description: Three accepted authorities: creator session, creator API key (ksk_…), or the envelope's OWN tracking token (ktt_…, from the create response's `tracking.token`) sent as the Authorization header. The tracking token is the read-only observer path (F-30.7): no account needed, full signer roster returned, exactly this envelope readable — any other envelope id answers the same 404 a stranger gets, and the token is refused (403 auth_tracking_scope) by every mutating or listing route.

post.summary: Void an active envelope and notify signers

get.summary: Download the creator's copy of the document
List the creator's uploaded documents (grouped by hash)
Signer review-page data (per-signer token from the invite email; no account)
Signer downloads their copy of the document (per-signer token)

post.summary: Mint a creator API key (the ksk_ value is returned EXACTLY once). Session-only: keys cannot manage keys.

201.description: Key minted (includes the raw key, once)

get.summary: List the creator's API keys (metadata only — never the key value)

delete.summary: Revoke an API key (takes effect on its next use)
